stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 2 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability of 90%. test 2 has a reliability between 77% and 86% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.977 to 0.986. Point estimate: 0.981.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 tests fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.1 × 0.23 = 0.023. P(works) = 0.977.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.1 × 0.14 = 0.014. P(works) = 0.986.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
